relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks a final vengeance campaign before Moses' death.
The command binds Moses' ending to war against Midian.
Israel is armed in the language of Divine vengeance.
A thousand from each tribe is summoned.
Twelve thousand go to war.
Phinehas accompanies them with holy vessels and alarm trumpets.
The campaign kills every Midianite male in the narrative.
Five kings and Balaam die by the sword.
Women, children, animals, and wealth are taken captive as spoil.
Cities and encampments are burned.
Human beings and animals are gathered into one category of prey.
The captives and goods are brought before Israel's leaders.
Moses, Eleazar, and the congregation meet the returning army.
Moses becomes angry—not over the killing, but because women remain alive.
He questions why every female captive was spared.
The women are blamed through the Peor interpretation and Balaam's counsel.
The command expands into killing boys and sexually experienced women.
Girls who have not known a male are retained for the victors.
Killers and captives remain outside camp for corpse purification.
Clothing and tools are also treated as contaminated by death.
Eleazar gives the purification rule for the war party.
Metals are named one by one.
Fire and water ritually process the objects of war.
After seven days the soldiers may reenter camp.
YHWH speaks the counting of captured lives and property.
The prey is numbered by leaders and priest.
Spoil is divided between fighters and congregation.
A levy is taken from humans and animals alike.
The warriors' levy goes to Eleazar.
The congregation's levy goes to Levite service.
Moses and Eleazar perform the commanded division.
The enormous flock count begins the inventory.
Cattle are counted.
Donkeys are counted.
Thirty-two thousand captive girls and women are counted as spoil.
Half the flock goes to the fighters.
A portion is marked as tribute to YHWH.
Cattle and their tribute are recorded.
Donkeys and their tribute are recorded.
Human captives and their tribute are recorded.
The priest receives the tribute.
The congregation's half is set apart.
Its flock total is recorded.
Its cattle total is recorded.
Its donkey total is recorded.
Its human captive total is recorded.
Levites receive one from fifty from humans and animals.
The military officers approach Moses.
They report that no Israelite soldier is missing.
They offer captured gold as covering for their own lives.
Moses and Eleazar receive the crafted gold.
The gold is weighed in sanctuary measure.
Individual soldiers also keep plunder.
The victors' gold becomes a memorial inside the tent.

contemplative_note: Numbers 31 is not mysticized into holiness. It is held under grief and protest: children are killed, women and girls are captured, people are counted as property, and plunder is sanctified from the victors' side. No spiritual reading may erase those human beings or repeat this violence.
